description: Stripe India went invite-only in May 2024 and still is (verified July 2026). New Indian-registered businesses cannot self-serve signup; Stripe Atlas is also invite-only for Indian founders. Wise is not an alternative: it has no checkout at all, and Indian Wise business accounts cannot hold balances (funds auto-convert to INR and sweep to an Indian bank).
resolution: Use Razorpay for Indian entities. It holds the RBI PA-CB cross-border licence (Dec 2025), covers domestic UPI/cards plus international cards in one integration, and supports physical-goods export. Keep the gateway behind the PaymentGateway interface in src/lib/commerce/types.ts so a future Stripe invite is a two-file swap.

name: Indian physical-goods export compliance prerequisites
description: Selling a physical product abroad from an Indian entity needs more than a payment gateway: an IEC (Importer-Exporter Code) from DGFT before you can legally ship, an LUT filed for zero-rated GST without paying IGST upfront, export proceeds landing in an AD Category-I bank, and an eBRC closing each shipment in RBI EDPMS within 15 months of the shipping bill.
